page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. The configuration includes 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing it in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block and large block bandwidths ranking in the bottom 25%. This memory limitation is reflected in database operations, where PassMark database benchmarks scored in the bottom 25%.

The hardware profile and benchmark results suggest a workload fit for general-purpose applications that benefit from high single-core CPU speeds but do not demand high memory bandwidth or local disk storage. Suitable tasks include static web serving, which achieved an average throughput of 6.54 GB/sec, and Redis key-value operations, yielding 5.9 million operations per second. It also supports light language model inference, processing 469.37 tokens per second for a small 135M model, though performance drops significantly on larger models. It is less suited for memory-bound database operations, software compilation, or heavy multi-threaded tasks due to the identified memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling constraints.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scores in the top 10%, alongside average multi-core and memory write performance. However, memory read bandwidth is a bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads like static web serving, Redis operations, and light single-threaded tasks, while it is less optimal for memory-intensive database operations or complex multi-threaded compilations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for web serving and light workloads despite memory bandwidth limitations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is an AMD EPYC-powered general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine delivering strong single-core AMD EPYC compute performance for standard web serving and utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and supports Premium Storage remote disks
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Exhibits lower memory read bandwidth, impacting database operations
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and light single-threaded tasks
